commands.name: cache
commands.description: List or clean libman cache contents

commands.name: clean
commands.description: Delete all files from the local machine's LibMan cache.

arguments.name: PROVIDER
arguments.description: Provider for which the cache files should be cleaned.

commands.name: list
commands.description:
Display a list of all libraries that are stored in the local machine’s LibMan cache.
Use "cache [command] --help" for more information about a command.

options.name: --files
options.description: List files that are cached for each library

commands.name: clean
commands.description: Deletes all library files defined in libman.json from the project

options.name: --verbosity
options.description:
Set the verbosity of output (eg. "normal", "detailed", "quiet")
Remarks:
Deletes any folders that become empty after this operation.

options.name: --set
options.description: Sets a key-value pair in the configuration store.  The option should be in the form key=value

options.name: --setEncrypted
options.description:
Sets a key with an encrypted value in the configuraiton store.  The option should be in the form key=plaintext
Remarks:
Settings cannot be read and stored in the same operation.
To remove a setting, set it to an empty string (--set key=).
Retrieving decrypted values for encrypted settings is not supported.

arguments.name: KEY
arguments.description: Name of the setting to retrieve

options.name: --default-provider
options.description: The provider to use if no provider is defined for a given library. (eg. “cdnjs”, “filesystem”)

options.name: --default-destination
options.description: The path, relative to the current directory, where library files should be installed if no destination is defined for a given library.

options.name: --useDefault
options.description: Use the default settings for the libman.json file

commands.name: install
commands.description: Add a library definition to the libman.json file, and download the library to the specified location

options.name: --provider
options.description: Provider to use (if not specified, the default provider will be used)

options.name: --destination
options.description: Location to install the library (if not specified, the default destination location will be used)

options.name: --files
options.description:
The files from the specified library to install (if not specified, all files from the library will be installed)
Remarks:
Initializes a libman.json if one does not exist
If no default provider exists, --provider option is required
If no default destination exists, --destination option is required
If no files are specified, the entire library is included

commands.name: restore
commands.description: Downloads all files from provider and saves them to specified destination

options.name: --verbosity
options.description:
Set the verbosity of output (eg. "normal", "detailed", "quiet")
Remarks:
Error if no libman.json in current folder
If a library specifies a provider, it will override the defaultProvider
If a library specifies a destination, it will override the defaultDestination

commands.name: uninstall
commands.description: Deletes all files for the specified library from their specified destination, then removes the specified library definition from libman.json

options.name: --verbosity
options.description:
Set the verbosity of output (eg. "normal", "detailed", "quiet")
Remarks:
Error if no libman.json in current folder
Error if specified library doesn't exist
If there's more than one library with the same libraryId, you'll be prompted to choose.

commands.name: update
commands.description:
Updates the specified library
Use "libman [command] --help" for more information about a command.

options.name: --pre
options.description: If specified, the latest pre-release version of the library will be downloaded (where applicable)

options.name: --to
options.description: The version to update the library to (needs complete libraryid for the provider)

options.name: --whatif
options.description:
Print what operations would be carried out, without making any changes.
Remarks:
Error if no libman.json in current folder
Error if specified library doesn't exist
If there's more than one library with the same libraryId, you'll be prompted to choose.

arguments.name: LIBRARYNAME
arguments.description: Name of the library to update (without the version).
